task_completion.visual_evidence: The 2N Access Commander dashboard is visible in a web browser, indicating successful login.
The 'Users' or 'Directory' section of the 2N Access Commander interface is open, displaying a list of users.

task_completion.description: Locate BuildingTech Solutions users with a 312 area code
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent has applied a search or filter in the user list for 'BuildingTech Solutions' or the '312' area code.

task_completion.description: Edit a user's phone number to change the area code
task_completion.visual_evidence: An edit screen, modal, or inline-edit field for a user is open, showing the phone number being modified to replace '312' with '872' while keeping the rest of the number intact.

task_completion.description: Update phone numbers for multiple target users
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent iterating through multiple users (e.g., Aisha Patel and others) to update their phone numbers, or applying a bulk update action.

task_completion.description: Verify that only the target users were updated
task_completion.visual_evidence: Final screenshots show the user list with '872' area codes for the target BuildingTech Solutions users, and explicitly show that users with 415, 773, or 847 area codes remain unchanged.

integrity.description: Agent used the web GUI to make changes
integrity.visual_evidence: Interactions occur within a web browser navigating the 2N Access Commander interface, not via terminal commands, curl, or database scripts.

integrity.description: Agent did not use Inspect Element to spoof results
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser's developer tools panel is not open, ensuring the UI changes are genuine and not DOM manipulation.
The agent is seen clicking 'Save' or confirmation buttons after editing phone numbers, and success notification toasts/messages are visible.

privileged_info_for_vlm: There are exactly 10 'BuildingTech Solutions' users (including 'Aisha Patel') that require the area code update from '312' to '872'. Ensure the agent updates all 10 users and that users with other area codes (e.g., 415, 773, 847) remain completely untouched.
